frameworks.name: two-node attractor network
frameworks.one_line: bistable attractor dynamics model decision memory in frontal cortex

frameworks.name: evidence integration
frameworks.one_line: integrator configuration tested as alternative account of bias

frameworks.name: evidence categorization
frameworks.one_line: categorization configuration tested as alternative account of bias

frameworks.name: decision memory
frameworks.one_line: postcategorization memory configuration of attractor network

core_question: Which attractor-network configuration of the rat FOF explains the stimulus-independent bias caused by causal perturbation?
key_assumptions: two-node attractor networks can flexibly capture distinct decision computations
FOF activity is causally involved in upcoming choice
configuration choice (integration vs categorization vs memory) is identifiable from perturbation effects
manipulations: alternative model configurations fit to data
key_findings: FOF pharmacological silencing produced a stimulus-independent bias
only the memory configuration of the attractor model accounts for this bias
memory model also captures optogenetic perturbation effects in the same task
model predicts a memory-duration-dependent deficit in a different task
interpretations: FOF supports a postcategorization decision-memory role in upcoming choices
bistable attractor dynamics provide a mechanistic account of FOF function
